message:
style: rustfmt pass across 100 files (updated assert!() macro wrapping rules)

Apply rustfmt with updated formatting rules across the entire workspace.
The 2024/2025 rustfmt edition changed how assert!() macros with
multi-line expressions are formatted — wrapping the expression inside
the assert parentheses rather than putting the closing paren on the same
line as the method call chain.

Changes are purely cosmetic with zero semantic impact:
- assert!(Expr { .. }.method()) split to multi-line assert!(\n    Expr { .. }\n        .method()\n)
- Single-line struct literals in tests split to multi-line
- FcpError pattern destructuring consolidated
- Trailing whitespace on blank lines within functions cleaned
- Raw strings r#"{}"# simplified to "{}" where unnecessary
- Import reordering (use statements sorted alphabetically)
- Closure argument formatting in paginate functions
- run_registry_test() closure bodies dedented one level

Affected: 15 connector error/types modules, 40+ crate source files,
integration tests, E2E tests, SDK schema tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
